titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Fliers for Punk Rock Music and New Wave Music Shows in the San Francisco Bay Area, California
Fliers for Punk Rock Music and New Wave Music Shows in the San Francisco Bay Area, California
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday September 17, 2019 at 03:12

did.unittitle: Fliers for Punk Rock Music and New Wave Music Shows in the San Francisco Bay Area, California

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Collection of 262 fliers for punk rock music and new wave music shows, tours, and related events in the San Francisco Bay Area, 1978-1986, as well as a few examples outside the region, 1981-2010. A local art professor collected the fliers that document portions of underground culture in the San Francisco Bay Area that influenced and reacted to the music scenes. The fliers are chiefly monochrome photocopies usually on 8.5-by-11-inch paper with graphical and textual elements. The guide to the collection identifies acts and venues as well as dates for shows and events.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Between the Covers on Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2012.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Fliers for Punk Rock Music and New Wave Music Shows in the San Francisco Bay Area, California, is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, may belong to the photographers or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: The Fliers for Punk Rock Music and New Wave Music Shows in the San Francisco Bay Area, California. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection of 262 fliers for punk rock music and new wave music shows, tours, and related events in the San Francisco Bay Area, 1978-1986, as well as a few examples outside the region, 1981-2010. A local art professor collected the fliers that document portions of underground culture in the San Francisco Bay Area that influenced and reacted to the music scenes. The fliers are chiefly monochrome photocopies usually on 8.5-by-11-inch paper with graphical and textual elements. The guide to the collection identifies acts and venues as well as dates for shows and events.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Venues, 1878-2010. II. Tours, 1980-1986.

subject.part: New wave music -- California -- San Francisco Bay Area
Nightclubs -- California -- San Francisco Bay Area
Punk culture -- California -- San Francisco Bay Area
Punk rock music -- California -- San Francisco Bay Area

did.unittitle: Venues

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fliers for shows chiefly in the San Francsco Bay Area of California, 1978-1986, as well as examples outside the region, 1981-2010. Venues are in San Francisco, California, except where noted. Each folder houses a single flier.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under 66 headings alphabetically for venues in the San Francisco Bay Area followed by headings for General Fliers and Venues Outside the San Francisco Bay Area.

did.unittitle: 10th Street Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10th Street Hall, 220 10th Street, active 1980-1981.

did.unittitle: Explosives, Flamin' Groovies, Kingsnake, and No Alternative at 10th Street Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by RRZ Promotions. Flier includes notices for other shows including Earl Zero at American Indian Center Hall and RedRockers at 10th Street Hall.

did.unittitle: Schedule for 10th Street Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by RRZ Promotions. Acts include Adolescents, Black Flag, D.O.A., Middle Class, and UK Decay.

did.unittitle: 544 Natoma Performance Gallery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 544 Natoma Performance Gallery, 544 Natoma Street, active 1982-1984.

did.unittitle: Rova Saxophone Quartet at 544 Natoma Performance Gallery
The Alchemy Lab

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Alchemy Lab, 1805 Geary, active 1983.

did.unittitle: Arkansaw Man, Kirk Charles Trio, and Slash at The Alchemy Lab
Sex and Science at The Alchemy Lab
Artists for Revolution in the Eighties Gallery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artists for Revolution in the Eighties (A.R.E.) Gallery, 1141 Market Street (1980), 513 Valencia Street (1981-1983), and 345B Folsom Street (1983-1984), 1980-1984

did.unittitle: Dickheads and Hymng Juniors at A.R.E. Gallery
Alamo Square Saloon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alamo Square Saloon was housed in Hotel Casa Loma, 600-610 Fillmore Street, 1980-1991.

did.unittitle: The Covers and Conan & Co., Sunday shows at Alamo Square Saloon
American Indian Center Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American Indian Center Hall, 225 Valencia Street, active 1970-1988.

did.unittitle: Ace and the Eights, The Blasters, and The Kingsnakes at American Indian Center Hall
The Hoovers and Neasden Knights at American Indian Center Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier reused a sheet from a catalog for Four-Phase Systems, San Bruno, California.

did.unittitle: The Invertebrates, The Job, and MX-80 Sound at American Indian Center Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by Emby.

did.unittitle: Box, Geza-X, and The Varve at American Indian Center Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by Emby.

did.unittitle: Arkansaw Man, Toiling Midgets, Wolvarines, and Woundz at American Indian Center Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by Emby.

did.unittitle: Mel Martin and Liz Lewis at the American Indian Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by Emby.

did.unittitle: Artists' Television Access

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artists' Television Access (ATA), 220 8th Street, 1984-1986, and 992 Valencia Street, since 1986.

did.unittitle: Masinenraum, New Generic, Problemist, and Tao Mao at Artists' Television Access

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by New Generic.

did.unittitle: Films at Artists' Television Access

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes films Autopia and Real Estate by "Spike," Butter Lust by David Hanawalt, Carte Blanche by Martinez/Collier Productions, Fuck Face by Julia Murray, I Live by the Freeway by Michael Freeman, and Reactor by Ron Ryder. Event included a performance by National Disgrace.

did.unittitle: Backdor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Backdor, also known as BackD.O.R., 1 Bartol Alley, active 1980.

did.unittitle: Schedule for Backdor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include Alternate Learning, Blake Quake and the Tremors, Das Blok, Dickheads, Eye Protection, Functionaries, Jane Doe, Le Masters, Mutants, Premier Night, Reggae Session, Sponges, Symptoms, Ultra Sheen, Whippets, and Zeros.

did.unittitle: Bannam Place Gallery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bannam Place Gallery, also known as Bannam Place Exhibition Space, 50A Bannam Place, 1983-1989.

did.unittitle: 2 Stars, Coloredblind James Experience, and The Rockets at Bannam Place Gallery
Bay Area Seating Service

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bay Area Seating Service was a ticket service agency in the San Francisco Bay Area, circa 1977-1997.

did.unittitle: Schedule for the Warfield Theatre and the Old Waldorf

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Double-sided flier with each side advertising a different venue including Old Waldorf and Warfield Theatre. Acts include B52's, Beat, Burning Spear, Bus Boys, English Beat, Flamin' Groovies, Jonny and the Distractions, Native, Psychedelic Furs, Purple Gang, Spys, The Talking Heads, The Titans, Toronto, The Tremblers, The Units, The Western Front, and VKTMS. Part of the Western Front Festival. Flier designed by Banett.

did.unittitle: Berkeley Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Berkeley Square, 1333 University Avenue in Berkeley, California, circa 1980-1997.

did.unittitle: Schedule for Berkeley Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include 07080, Boys Life, Cha Cha Billy, Chrome Dinette, Dangerous Curves, Delta 5, Elements of Style, Fashion Theater, Flaming Groovies, Hoovers, Ironics, Jim Carroll, Krayons, Lloyds, Mr. Clean, Mr. Clean, Naked City, No Sisters, Nu-Models, Oquisha, Paradox, Passions, Peter Bilt & the Expressions, Peter Thomas, Portables, Portables, Questions, Reggae Fever, Research Library, Snakepit, Squares, SVT, The Blok, Toiling Midgets, Tools, Untouchables, Vandals, Verbs, Video Rouge, Voice Farm, Whippet, and X-iles.

did.unittitle: Schedule for Berkeley Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include 84 Rooms, Barry Beam, Bob, Boy's Life, Breakouts, Chrome Dinette, Contrabantiempo, Crime, F.O.C., Factrix, Glenn Branca, Hard Attack, Harvey, Hoovers, Imposters, Informants, Jars, Joe Richards, Krayons, Lloyd's, Los Microwaves, Middle Class, Minimal Man, Mr. Clean, Peter Bilt & The Expressions, Pure Honey, Rank and File, Sponges, St. Regis, Strap D'Etro, Surface Music, SVT, Teleport, The Balls, The Blok, The Tong, Verbs, VKTMS, Washed Up & Shady, Wayne Sloan, Wild Remain, Witnesses, and Young Adults.

did.unittitle: Schedule for Berkeley Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include Bush Tetras, Crime, Earl Zero, Elements of Style, Geza-X, Hoovers, Kingsnakes, Les Microwaves, Neutrinoz, No Alternative, Pure Honey, Raybeats, Reggae Fever, Reggies, Roy Loney and the Phantom Movers, Sick Pleasure, Silhouette, Squares, Titans, Unreal Band, Untouchables, Weirdos, Whippets, Wimps, Wolvarines, and Zeff & the Ravers.

did.unittitle: Schedule for Berkeley Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include 07080, Appliances, Bad Attitude, Boy's Life, Chrome Dinette, Contrabandtiempo, Earl Zero, Elements of Style, Hoovers, Indoor Life, Jayne Doe, Jet Set, Los Microwaves, Our Daughter's Wedding, Peter Bilt and the Expressions, Pre Fix, Pure Honey, Rebel Rockets, Surface Music, SVT, The Fire, The Natives East Bay Mud, Tong, Spies, Unreal Band, Untouchables, Verbs, Vicious Pigeons, Weirdos, Wolvarines, and Woundz.

did.unittitle: 07080, The Hoovers, and Indoor Life, at Berkeley Square
Chrome Dinette, Elements of Style, and The Valkays at Berkeley Square
B Team, Blackouts, and The Paris Working at Berkeley Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier designed by "Audrey."

did.unittitle: Schedule for Berkeley Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include 84 Rooms, Barry Beam, Black Flag, Chrome Dinettes, Cowboys, Crime, Dangerous Curves, Deakin, Earl Zero, Eddie and the Subtitles, Elements of Style, F.O.C., Factrix, Gear, Hard-Attack, Hostages, Humans, Ironics, Jayne Doe, Johanna Whent, Krayons, Los Microwaves, Mixers, Modernettes, Naked City, Name, Neasden Knights, Nervous Gender, No Sisters, Nu-Models, Our Daughter's Wedding, Peter Bilt and the Expressions, Portables, Record Party!, Reggae Fever, Snakefinger, The Blok, The Lewd, The Pop, Two-Tones, Ultrasheen, Untouchables, Val-Kays, VKTMS, Wall of Voodoo, Whippets, and Young Adults.

did.unittitle: Boarding House

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Boarding House, 960 Bush Street, 1971-1980, and 901 Columbus, 1980-1982.

did.unittitle: Daniel Amos and Heart Attack at Boarding House

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Part of the Doppelganger Tour. Produced by VCM and Brian Martin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Booker T. Washington Community Center, 800 Presidio Avenue, since 1923.

did.unittitle: Crucifix Trial at Booker T. Washington Community Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Show included a screening of the film, Army .

did.unittitle: California Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: California Hall, 625 Polk Street, since 1912.

did.unittitle: D.O.A., Siouxsie and the Banshees, and Wounds at California Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier chiefly advertises an appearance at Universal Records and Tapes.

scopecontent.p: Cento Cedar Cinema, 38 Cedar Alley, 1965-1988.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Club Foot, 2520 3rd Street, 1980-1985.

scopecontent.p: Club Nine, 399 9th Street, 1986-1987.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Release party for the Piglatin extended play, My Hands Go Blind .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dreamland, 715 Harrison, 1980-1987.

did.unittitle: No Sisters and Wolvarines at Dreamland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notice of another show with Flamin' Groovies and Lenny Kaye Connection.

did.unittitle: Flamin' Groovies, Lenny Kaye Connection, and The Ramones at Dreamland
Chrome Dinette and Mutants at Dreamland
Duboce Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Duboce Park, an urban park bounded by Scott Street and Steiner Street, established 1902.

did.unittitle: Lloyds, SVT, and Terry Hutchison at Duboce Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by Castro Street Fair, Inc.

did.unittitle: The EndUp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The EndUp, 401 6th St, since 1973.

did.unittitle: "Church" with DJ Glenn Judd and DJ Ron Baer at The EndUp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows began at 6:00 am on Saturdays and Sundays.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Farm, 1499 Potrero Avenue, 1974-1987.

did.unittitle: Agent Orange, Screaming Sirens, and Sea Hags at The Farm
Circle Jerks and Crucifix at The Farm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by RRZ Promotions.

did.unittitle: Headquarters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Headquarters, 683 Clementina Street, 1980-1982

did.unittitle: After hours dancing at Headquarters
Hotel Utah Saloon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hotel Utah Saloon, 500 4th Street, established 1908.

did.unittitle: Arms Akimbo at Hotel Utah Saloon
Outskirts at Hotel Utah Saloon
I-Beam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I-Beam, 1748 Haight Street, 1977-1994.

did.unittitle: Keith Hollings and Psychic Surgery at I-Beam
Eye Protection and Valkays at I-Beam
The Job with The Offs at I-Beam
DJ Alan Robinson, Modern Machine, and Our Daughter's Wedding at I-Beam
VKTMS at I-Beam
Timmy and the Sluts with VKTMS at I-Beam
DJ Alan Robinson, Eye Protection, and Pylon at I-Beam
Holly and the Italians with Peter Bilt at I-Beam
Justin Herman Plaza

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Justin Herman Plaza, renamed Embarcadero Plaza in 2017, is a plaza near the intersection of Market Street and Embarcadero opened in 1972.

did.unittitle: Factrix and Flipper at Justin Herman Plaza

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier identified the site as "Valin Court Plaza" after the Vaillancourt Fountain , sometimes called Quebec libre! , a fountain in the plaza designed by Armand Vaillancourt and completed in 1971.

did.unittitle: Keystone Berkeley

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Keystone Berkeley, 2119 University Avenue in Berkeley, 1972-1984.

did.unittitle: Mercenaries, Professionals, Ready Mayhem, and Undead at Keystone Berkeley
KPFA Radio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: KPFA (94.1 FM), radio station in Berkeley, since 1949.

did.unittitle: Schedule for Reggae Day and Zimbabwe Day programming on KPFA radio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Double-sided flier.

did.unittitle: Le Disque

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Le Disque, 1840 Haight, active 1980-1982.

did.unittitle: Janos at Le Disque
Hostages, Little Death, Nobody Famous, and Stupeds at Le Disque
The Imposters, Interference, and Teleport at Le Disque
The Act and The Hoovers at Le Disque
Exposure, Red Asphalt, and The Varve at Le Disque

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Live recording of the show.

did.unittitle: Dark Side of Pop, Passion Kick, and The Varve at Le Disque

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mabuhay Gardens, 443 Broadway, 1976-1988.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by Dirksen-Miller Productions.

Christian Death, Half Japanese, Psychic TV, The Farm, and Velvet Monkeys at Mabuhay Gardens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced by RRZ Promotions.

did.unittitle: D.O.A., False Idols, Tenants, and VKTMS at Mabuhay Gardens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Benefit show for Videowest. Produced by Dirksen-Miller Productions. Flier designed by Roger I. Reyes. Photography by Ellen Baragon.

did.unittitle: Funktionaries, Humans, Union of Concerned Comics (also known as the Commies), and VKTMS at Mabuhay Gardens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Election Day Eve show hosted by Wes Nisker. Included screening of film, "Reagan's Golden Turkeys." Produced by Dirksen-Miller Productions. Flier designed by Roger I. Reyes.

did.unittitle: Alienation, Undersongs, Verbs, and Witnesses at Mabuhay Gardens

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Show was at the Rothwell Center.

did.unittitle: Mission Rock Resort

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mission Rock Resort, 817 China Basin Street, 1975-1997.

did.unittitle: Hot Links at Mission Rock Resort

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Benefit show, "Anti 8 Ball," for the Californians for Responsible Law Enforcement campaign against Proposition 8.

did.unittitle: Music Concourse, Golden Gate Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Music Concourse is a landscaped basin between the California Academy of Sciences and the de Young Museum within Golden Gate Park built in 1900.

did.unittitle: Amerikan Dream and Fried Abortions at the Music Concourse, Golden Gate Park
Nightbreak

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nightbreak, 1821 Haight Street, active 1985-1987.

scopecontent.p: Flier desined by Jimi Modern.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include The Blasters, Captain Beefheart, D.N.A., Go-Go's, Human Hands, Snakefinger, Wall of Voodoo, and X.

scopecontent.p: Acts include Joe "King" Carrasco, Contractions, The Brains, D.O.A., No Sisters, Red Rockers, Romeo Void, Secret Affair, SVT, Tazmanian Devils, U2, and VKTMS. Flier designed by Peter Soe, Jr.

Schedule for On Broadway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include 100 Flowers, Animal Things, Circle Jerks, Middle Class, Mutants, Red Cross, and Twisted Roots. Produced by RRZ Promotions.

did.unittitle: Schedule for On Broadway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include Animal Things, Bad Religion, Circle Jerks, Intensified Chaos, Salvation Army, Stir Ups, T.S.O.L., and Twisted Roots.

did.unittitle: Schedule for On Broadway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include Angst, Descendants, Minutemen, Saccharine Trust, and T.S.O.L. Produced by RRZ Promotions.

did.unittitle: Schedule for On Broadway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include Arkansaw Man, C.F. Players, Church Police, Circle Jerks, Meat Puppets, Modern Torture, My Sin, Red Kross, Social Distortion, Super Heroines, T.S.O.L., The Brat, and Toiling Midgets. Produced by RRZ Promotions.

did.unittitle: Hazard, His Majesty's Ghost, and Sex Dates at On Broadway
Black Dolls, Minimal Man, Skeletons, and Toiling Midgets at On Broadway
Schedule for On Broadway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include Code of Honor, Black Athletes, Tales of Terror, Vacant, Poor White Trash, Swinging Possums, Stirrups, Rockin' Daddys, Golden Voice, and The Exploited. Produced by RRZ Promotions and Golden Voice. Flier designed by Freak Posters.

did.unittitle: Schedule for On Broadway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include Agent Orange, Black Athletes, Channel 3, Circle Jerks, Code of Honor, Fang, Fifth Column, Fuck Ups , Los Olvidadas, Minor Threat, Offigies, Rank & File, and T.S.O.L. Produced by RRZ Promotions.

did.unittitle: Defeatist Attitude, Fade to Black, and My Sin at On Broadway
Schedule for On Broadway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include 7 Seconds, Battalion of Saints, Black Flag, Channel 3, Crucifix, Exitus, Fear, Los Olvidados, Murder, Sabotage Control, Silvertone, The Untouchables, and The Violent Femmes. Produced by RRZ Promotions.

did.unittitle: Violent Femmes at On Broadway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Subterranean Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subterranean Records, founded 1979.

did.unittitle: Schedule by Subterranean Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include 10th Street Hall, Barrington Hall, Berkeley Square, Mabuhay Gardens, Old Waldorf, San Jose Unitarian Church, Savoy Tivoli, Sound of Music, and Sproul Plaza at the University of California, Berkeley. Flier designed by Kate Mitchell, Gettafix Graphics. Acts include B People, Black Flag, Breakouts, Charles McMahon, Dez, Dioxin, DOA, Flipper, Fried Abortion, I.Y., Jars, Nervous Gender, No Alternative, Penis Envy & the Snap-on Tools, Pre-fix, Sick Pleasure, Society Dog, Spikes, Tanks, Toiling Midgets, Ultrasheen, Undead, Uns, Vandals, War Zone, Weirdos, Wolvarines, and Woundz.

did.unittitle: Schedule by Subterranean Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include 10th Street Hall, Barrington Hall, Berkeley Square, I-Beam, Le Disque, Mabuhay Gardens, Pier 39, Sound of Music, Target Video, University California Berkeley (Counter Graduation Ball), and Veterans Hall (Berkeley). Acts include ALA, Animals Things, Black Flag, Breakouts, Collectors, Drugs, Earl Zero, Flamin Groovies, Flipper, Ghouls, Jars, Lewd Mutants, Naked Lady Wrestlers, Revenge, Seven Seconds, Sluts a Go-Go, Target Video, Toiling Midgets, Wolvarines, and Woundz.

Venues Outside the San Francisco Bay Area
Kyoto University, Japan

Genbaku Onanīzu (Atomic Onanīzu), Masutābēshon (Masturbation), Nashi (Nashi), Sutā Kurabu (Star Club), Tīn'eiji Rejisutansu (Teenage Resistance), and Za Conchinentaru Kizzu (The Continental Kids) at Seibu Kōdō (West Auditorium), Kyoto University, Kyoto, Japan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flier in Japanese. The show took place on the Taiiku no Hi (Health and Sports Day) holiday.

did.unittitle: Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acts include Joey Wilson, King of Siam, NNB, Pleasure Dotz, Roustabout Trio, The Front Lines, The Pets, and Waitresses at East Side Club.

did.unittitle: Tours

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fliers for tours chiefly in the San Francisco Bay Area of California, 1980-1986. Venues are in San Francisco, California, except where noted. Each folder houses a single flier.

did.unittitle: Agent Orange tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include The Farm, Mabuhay Gardens, and The Rock. Supporting and partner acts include Christ on Parade, D.I., House of Wheels, Peter and the Test Tube Babie, Rhythm Pigs, Sea Hags, and The English Dogs.

did.unittitle: Angry Samoans tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues including Old Waldorf and Sound of Music.

did.unittitle: Appliances tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Le Disque and Old Waldorf. Supporting and partner acts include Los Microwaves, Romeo Void, and U2.

did.unittitle: Barry Beam, Mod Mach, and See Spot
Black Dolls tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include On Broadway and Mabuhay Gardens. Supporting and partner acts Minimal Man, Toiling Midgets, Skeletons, and Screen of Dreams

did.unittitle: Chrome Dinette tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Berkeley Square, Mabuhay Gardens, Old Waldorf, The Palms Café, Savoy Tivoli, and Sproul Plaza at the University of California, Berkeley.

did.unittitle: Contractions tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Old Waldorf, Ollie's, and Mabuhay Gardens. Supporting and partner acts include 07080, Barry Beam, Hostages, Interference, Lifers, Saucers, and Tasmanian Devils. Flier designed by Inkdragon with photograph by F. Stop Fitzgerald.

did.unittitle: Cut Up tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Berkeley Square, Mabuhay Gardens, Rock City, San Francisco Art Institute, Savoy Tivoli, Sound of Music, The Hell Hole, and Valencia Tool & Die.

did.unittitle: Dot 3 tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues includue Berkeley Square and Mabuhay Gardens. Supporting and partner acts include Captain Crunch, Minus One, and Steven Moore Project.

did.unittitle: Echo Recon tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Le Disque and Sound of Music. Flier designed by Marlo.

did.unittitle: Fools Brigade tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Heaven's Gate, Mabuhay Gardens, Sound of Music.

did.unittitle: G.B.H. and Metal Church tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Supporting acts include 7 Seconds, and Verbal Abuse. Produced by Clear & Distinct Ideas, RRZ Promotions, and Golden Voice. Also advertises a show by Social Distortion on June 28, 1985.

did.unittitle: The Hoovers tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Berkeley Square, Keystone Berkeley, Le Disque, Mabuhay Gardens, The Palms, Savoy Tivoli, Sound of Music, and University of California, Berkeley Plaza. Tour to support the album, "Skin and Blisters."

did.unittitle: The Hoovers tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Joshua's (San Jose), Keystone Palo Alto, Mabuhay Gardens, Niles Station (Fremont), and The Palms. Supporting and partner acts include 07080, The Immortals, The Lloyds, and Reggies as well as a show with the Honeymooners to benefit the San Jose Institute of Contemporary Art.

did.unittitle: Interference tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Le Disque, Ollies, and Mabuhay Gardens. Supporting and partner acts include Contractions, Elements of Style, and Varve.

did.unittitle: House of Pants tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Keystone Berkeley and Mabuhay Gardens. Other acts include Katherine and Western Front.

did.unittitle: Joan Jett & the Blackhearts tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Keystone Berkeley, Keystone Palo Alto, and The Stone. Photograph on Flier of the band by Stanley Greene.

did.unittitle: The Job tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Mabuhay Gardens and Le Disque. Supporting and partner acts include Red Asphalt.

did.unittitle: Joe Higgs tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Keystone Berkeley and The Stone

did.unittitle: Lenny Kaye and Silvertone tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Keystone Berkeley and The Stone

did.unittitle: The Lloyds tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Alpen Glow, Berkeley Square, I-Beam, Keystone Berkeley, Le Disque, Old Waldorf, Rock City, The Palms, and The Stone. Photograph on flier by Michael Jang.

did.unittitle: The Middle Class tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Mabuhay Gardens, Sound of Music, and Berkeley Square. Supporting and partner acts include De-Detroit, Rank and File, Wounds, and Zeros.

did.unittitle: Pre Fix tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Berkeley Square, The Hell Hole, Mabuhay Gardens, Rock City, San Francisco Art Institute, Savoy Tivoli, Sound of Music, and Valencia Tool & Die.

did.unittitle: Rude Boy film

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include York Theatre, Fairfax Theatre, and El Rey Theatre.

did.unittitle: Sea Hags tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include I-Beam and Mabuhay Gardens. Supporting and partner acts include Celebrity Skin, The Cramps, and Das Damen.

did.unittitle: The Stranglers tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Keystone Palo Alto and The Stone

did.unittitle: Sweet Tommy Band tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include the "Erotic, Exotic Ball" at California Hall as well as Mabuhay Gardens, Palms Café, Rock City, and Sound of Music.

did.unittitle: Sweet Tommy Band tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Mabuhay Gardens, Palms Café, and The Stone.

did.unittitle: Vauxhall tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Berkeley Square, Le Disque, Sound of Music. Supporting and partner acts include Verbs and Neutrinoz.

did.unittitle: Vauxhall tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Mabuhay Gardens, Sound of Music, and The Stone. Supporting and partner acts include Surface Music and Flamin' Groovies.

did.unittitle: The Visitors tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Alpen Glo in Hayward, Galactica 2000 in Sacramento, Hotel St. Claire in San Jose, Mabuhay Gardens, The New Oasis, The Palms, and The Stone. Supporting and partner acts include The Dickies, Kid Courage, The Spectators, The Squares, SVT, The Visitors, and Zo Bop.

did.unittitle: The Visitors tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Alpen Glo in Hayward, Berkeley Square, Joshua's in San Jose, Mabuhay Gardens, Niles Station in Fremont, and The Palms. Supporting acts include Boxboys, Chris Hamburger, Mojo, No Sisters, St. Regis, Sweet Tommy, The Hoovers, The Immortals, and The Squares.

did.unittitle: Wolvarines tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Venues include Mabuhay Gardens and San Francisco Art Institute. Supporting and partner acts include Barry Beam, Dickheads, and Frank Hymng.
